pi_items.source: DICOM analysis: CT Cranium 0051 dataset contains brain tissue in the -100 HU range (confirmed by voxel analysis across 108 slices)
DICOM analysis: CT Cranium 0051 dataset contains brain tissue up to 80 HU (confirmed by voxel analysis across 108 slices)
DICOM analysis: CT Cranium 0051 dataset contains compact bone structures at >= 600 HU. Slice analysis shows 5872-11339 bone voxels per slice in central slices
DICOM analysis: CT Cranium 0051 dataset contains periorbital fat tissue with maximum HU <= -20 (confirmed by voxel analysis across multiple slices)
DICOM analysis: CT Cranium 0051 dataset contains periorbital fat tissue with minimum HU >= -300 (confirmed by voxel analysis across 108 slices)

Task specification and verifier.py: Three distinct tissue masks required (brain, bone, fat)
Task specification and verifier.py: Three separate STL exports required (brain_tissue.stl, skull_bone.stl, periorbital_fat.stl)
Task specification (README.md and verifier.py): At least 5 linear measurements required to document inter-tissue distances

privileged_info_summary: The CT Cranium dataset (series 0051) is real DICOM data from the official InVesalius GitHub release containing 108 axial slices with pixel spacing of 0.478516 x 0.478516 mm and slice thickness of 2.0 mm. The dataset contains all three required tissue types with the specified Hounsfield Unit ranges: brain parenchyma in the -100 to 80 HU range, compact cortical bone at >=600 HU, and periorbital fat tissue in the -300 to -20 HU range. The task requires creation of three separate segmentation masks and STL surface exports with documented inter-tissue distance measurements saved to an InVesalius project file.
